summary: ByteDance officially launched Seed 2.0 and separately exposed Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena, marking a concrete new foundation-model release from one of China’s largest AI investors. Google also shipped AI Edge Gallery, an official mobile app for running Gemma 4 and Gemma 3-family models on-device, while AgentScope 1.0 emerged as a notable agent framework release aimed at large-scale multi-agent simulation and production agentic applications.

top_topics.title: Seed 2.0 Official Launch
top_topics.summary: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0. The announcement marks the formal release of the company's new-generation foundation model.

items.title: ByteDance Seed 2027 Foundation Model Campus Recruitment Opens
items.summary: ByteDance Seed announced that its 2027 Foundation Model Campus Recruitment program is now open, including internship opportunities. This is a company recruitment announcement for students interested in joining the foundation model team.

items.title: Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview Released on Arena
items.summary: ByteDance Seed announced the release of Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena. The post indicates a preview availability of the model for public evaluation or testing.

items.title: Seed 2.0 Official Launch
items.summary: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0. This is a formal model release announcement for the company's new generation foundation model.

items.title: Seed Prover 1.5 Announced
items.summary: ByteDance Seed announced Seed Prover 1.5, describing it as an advanced mathematical reasoning model built on a novel agentic architecture. The release emphasizes stronger reasoning capabilities for math-related tasks.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0. The announcement marks the formal release of the company's new-generation foundation model.
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: ByteDance’s official model launch is a tangible signal of continued frontier-model investment from a hyperscale consumer platform with the capital and distribution to matter.
top_topics.HEADLINE: ByteDance Ships Seed 2.0 and Opens Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: ByteDance Seed formally announced Seed 2.0 as its new-generation foundation model in an official company release.
The company also made Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview available on Arena, giving the market an early public evaluation path rather than a purely closed announcement.
The same announcement stream included ByteDance Seed’s 2027 foundation-model campus recruiting push, indicating the release is paired with ongoing talent expansion rather than a one-off demo.

top_topics.title: AgentScope 1.0: A Developer-Centric Framework for Building Agentic Applications
top_topics.summary: AgentScope 1.0 is a developer-focused framework for building agentic applications. It provides infrastructure for combining LLM reasoning with tool use in real-world workflows.

items.title: Very Large-Scale Multi-Agent Simulation in AgentScope
items.summary: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have opened new avenues for applying multi-agent systems in very large-scale simulations. However, there remain several challenges when conducting multi-agent simulations with existing platforms, such as limited scalability and low efficiency, unsatisfied agent diversity, and effort-intensive management processes. To address these challenges, we develop several new features and components for AgentScope, a user-friendly multi-agent platform, enhancing its convenience and flexibility for supporting very large-scale multi-agent simulations. Spec...

metadata.ai_summary: Enhancements to the AgentScope platform improve scalability, efficiency, and ease of use for large-scale multi-agent simulations through distributed mechanisms, flexible environments, and user-friendly tools.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have opened new avenues for applying multi-agent systems in very large-scale simulations. However, there remain several challenges when conducting multi-agent simulations with existing platforms, such as limited scalability and low efficiency, unsat...

items.title: AgentScope 1.0: A Developer-Centric Framework for Building Agentic Applications
items.summary: Driven by rapid advancements of Large Language Models (LLMs), agents are empowered to combine intrinsic knowledge with dynamic tool use, greatly enhancing their capacity to address real-world tasks. In line with such an evolution, AgentScope introduces major improvements in a new version (1.0), towards comprehensively supporting flexible and efficient tool-based agent-environment interactions for building agentic applications. Specifically, we abstract foundational components essential for agentic applications and provide unified interfaces and extensible modules, enabling developers to eas...

metadata.ai_summary: AgentScope enhances agentic applications by providing flexible tool-based interactions, unified interfaces, and advanced infrastructure based on the ReAct paradigm, supporting efficient and safe development and deployment.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Driven by rapid advancements of Large Language Models (LLMs), agents are empowered to combine intrinsic knowledge with dynamic tool use, greatly enhancing their capacity to address real-world tasks. In line with such an evolution, AgentScope introduces major improvements in a new version (1.0), t...

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: AgentScope 1.0 is a developer-focused framework for building agentic applications. It provides infrastructure for combining LLM reasoning with tool use in real-world workflows.
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: Agent infrastructure is becoming a strategic layer, and AgentScope 1.0 targets teams building multi-agent systems that need scalability beyond prototype orchestration.
top_topics.HEADLINE: AgentScope 1.0 Releases as a Developer Framework for Agentic Applications
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: AgentScope 1.0 is positioned as a developer-centric framework for building agentic applications, not just a research demo for isolated benchmarks.
Associated work on 'Very Large-Scale Multi-Agent Simulation in AgentScope' highlights scalability and efficiency as core design goals, addressing bottlenecks in existing multi-agent platforms.
The framework explicitly targets agents that combine LLM reasoning with dynamic tool use, aligning with the current enterprise shift from chat interfaces to workflow-executing systems.

top_topics.title: Google AI Edge Gallery
top_topics.summary: Google AI Edge Gallery is an official Google app highlighted for running AI models on-device. The post describes it as a mobile app available through Apple's App Store.

items.title: Google AI Edge Gallery
items.summary:
Google AI Edge Gallery

Terrible name, really great app: this is Google's official app for running their Gemma 4 models (the E2B and E4B sizes, plus some members of the Gemma 3 family) directly on your iPhone.

It works really well. The E2B model is a 2.54GB download and is both fast and genuinely useful.

The app also provides "ask questions about images" and audio transcription (up to 30s) with the two small Gemma 4 models, and has an interesting "skills" demo which demonstrates tool calling against eight different interactive widgets, each implemented as an HTML page (though sadly the source code is not visible): interactive-map, kitchen-adventure, calculate-has...

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: Google AI Edge Gallery is an official Google app highlighted for running AI models on-device. The post describes it as a mobile app available through Apple's App Store.
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: Google is productizing local inference on consumer devices, which matters for privacy-sensitive deployments, lower serving costs, and broader adoption of Gemma as an edge AI stack.
top_topics.HEADLINE: Google Launches AI Edge Gallery for On-Device Gemma 4 and Gemma 3 Models
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: AI Edge Gallery is Google’s official app for running Gemma models directly on phones, according to the cited App Store listing and analysis.
The app supports Gemma 4 E2B and E4B variants, plus selected Gemma 3-family models, making it a concrete distribution channel for Google’s open model line.
The release shifts Gemma from a model family developers download manually to a packaged end-user experience, which could accelerate experimentation with offline and low-latency AI use cases.

top_topics.title: Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with PagedAttention
top_topics.summary: This paper presents PagedAttention, a memory-management approach for serving large language models more efficiently. It focuses on improving throughput by handling KV-cache memory more effectively during batched inference.

items.title: Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with PagedAttention
items.summary: High throughput serving of large language models (LLMs) requires batching sufficiently many requests at a time. However, existing systems struggle because the key-value cache (KV cache) memory for each request is huge and grows and shrinks dynamically. When managed inefficiently, this memory can be significantly wasted by fragmentation and redundant duplication, limiting the batch size. To address this problem, we propose PagedAttention, an attention algorithm inspired by the classical virtual memory and paging techniques in operating systems. On top of it, we build vLLM, an LLM serving sys...

metadata.ai_summary: PagedAttention algorithm and vLLM system enhance the throughput of large language models by efficiently managing memory and reducing waste in the key-value cache.
metadata.spotlight_comment: High throughput serving of large language models (LLMs) requires batching sufficiently many requests at a time. However, existing systems struggle because the key-value cache (KV cache) memory for each request is huge and grows and shrinks dynamically. When managed inefficiently, this memory can...

top_topics.title: Aligning Progress and Feasibility: A Neuro-Symbolic Dual Memory Framework for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
top_topics.summary: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong potential in long-horizon decision-making tasks, such as embodied manipulation and web interaction. However, agents frequently struggle with endless trial-and-error loops or deviate from the main objective in complex environments. We attribute these failures to two fundamental errors: global Progress Drift and local Feasibility Violation. Existing methods typically attempt to address both issues simultaneously using a single paradigm. However, these two challenges are fundamentally distinct: the former relies on fuzzy semantic planning,...

items.title: Aligning Progress and Feasibility: A Neuro-Symbolic Dual Memory Framework for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
items.summary: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong potential in long-horizon decision-making tasks, such as embodied manipulation and web interaction. However, agents frequently struggle with endless trial-and-error loops or deviate from the main objective in complex environments. Existing methods typically attempt to address both issues simultaneously using a single paradigm. However, these two challenges are fundamentally distinct: the former relies on fuzzy semantic planning,...

metadata.comment: Decouples long-horizon agent memory into semantic progress memory and symbolic feasibility memory for storing and validating past experience.

topics.title: Suno is a music copyright nightmare
topics.summary: AI music platform Suno's policy is that it does not permit the use of copyrighted material. You can upload your own tracks to remix or set your original lyrics to AI-generated music. But, it's supposed to recognize and stop you from using other people's songs and lyrics. Now, no system is perfect, but it turns […]

topics.title: Eight years of wanting, three months of building with AI
topics.summary:
Eight years of wanting, three months of building with AI

Lalit Maganti provides one of my favorite pieces of long-form writing on agentic engineering I've seen in ages.

They spent eight years thinking about and then three months building syntaqlite, which they describe as "high-fidelity devtools that SQLite deserves".

The goal was to provide fast, robust and comprehensive linting and verifying tools for SQLite, suitable for use in language servers and other development tools - a parser, formatter, and verifier for SQLite queries. I've found myself wanting this kind of thing in the pa...

topics.SHORT_COMMENT:
Eight years of wanting, three months of building with AI

Lalit Maganti provides one of my favorite pieces of long-form writing on agentic engineering I've seen in ages.

They spent eight years thinking about and then three months building syntaqlite, which they describe as "high-fidelity devtools that SQLite deserves".

The goal was to provide fast, robust and comprehensive linting and verifying tools for SQLite, suitable for use in language servers and other development tools - a parser, formatter, and verifier for SQLite queries. I've found myself wanting this kind of thing in the pa...

topics.HEADLINE: Eight years of wanting, three months of building with AI

topics.title: Show HN: Gemma Gem – AI model embedded in a browser – no API keys, no cloud
topics.summary: Gemma Gem is a Chrome extension that runs Google's Gemma 4 2B model locally in the browser using WebGPU. The tool can interact with webpages by reading content, taking screenshots, and clicking elements without API keys or cloud inference.

items.title: Show HN: Gemma Gem – AI model embedded in a browser – no API keys, no cloud
items.summary: Gemma Gem is a Chrome extension that loads Google's Gemma 4 (2B) through WebGPU in an offscreen document and gives it tools to interact with any webpage: read content, take screenshots, click elements, type text, scroll, and run JavaScript. You get a small chat overlay on every page. Ask it about the page and it (usually) figures out which tools to call. It has a thinking mode that shows chain-of-thought reasoning...

topics.title: Show HN: Contrapunk – Real-time counterpoint harmony from guitar input
topics.summary: Contrapunk is a real-time music tool that generates counterpoint harmonies from guitar, MIDI, or keyboard input. The project was shared on Hacker News as a demo or early release.

items.title: Show HN: Contrapunk – Real-time counterpoint harmony from guitar input
items.summary: Hi HN, I built Contrapunk because I wanted to play guitar and hear counterpoint harmonies generated in real-time. It takes audio from your guitar, MIDI player or your computer keyboard and generates harmony voices that follow counterpoint rules to generate harmonies. You can choose the key you would like to improvise/play in and the voice leading style and which part of the harmony you would like to play as, as we...

topics.title: The AI Scientist-v2: Workshop-Level Automated Scientific Discovery via Agentic Tree Search
topics.summary: The AI Scientist-v2 is an agentic system for automated scientific discovery using tree search. The paper claims the system can produce workshop-level scientific outputs end to end.

items.title: The AI Scientist-v2: Workshop-Level Automated Scientific Discovery via Agentic Tree Search
items.summary: AI is increasingly playing a pivotal role in transforming how scientific discoveries are made. We introduce The AI Scientist-v2, an end-to-end agentic system capable of producing the first entirely AI generated peer-review-accepted workshop paper. This system iteratively formulates scientific hypotheses, designs and executes experiments, analyzes and visualizes data, and autonomously authors scientific manuscripts. Compared to its predecessor (v1, Lu et al., 2024 arXiv:2408.06292), The AI Scientist-v2 eliminates the reliance on human-authored code templates, generalizes effectively across d...

metadata.ai_summary: The AI Scientist-v2 autonomously proposes hypotheses, performs experiments, analyzes data, and writes peer-reviewed scientific papers, marking the first fully AI-generated paper accepted by a conference.
metadata.spotlight_comment: AI is increasingly playing a pivotal role in transforming how scientific discoveries are made. topics.title: Finding Belief Geometries with Sparse Autoencoders
topics.summary: Understanding the geometric structure of internal representations is a central goal of mechanistic interpretability. Prior work has shown that transformers trained on sequences generated by hidden Markov models encode probabilistic belief states as simplex-shaped geometries in their residual stream, with vertices corresponding to latent generative states. Whether large language models trained on naturalistic text develop analogous geometric representations remains an open question. We introduce a pipeline for discovering candidate simplex-structured subspaces in transformer representations,...

topics.title: Learning the Signature of Memorization in Autoregressive Language Models
topics.summary: All prior membership inference attacks for fine-tuned language models use hand-crafted heuristics (e.g., loss thresholding, Min-K\%, reference calibration), each bounded by the designer's intuition. We introduce the first transferable learned attack, enabled by the observation that fine-tuning any model on any corpus yields unlimited labeled data, since membership is known by construction. This removes the shadow model bottleneck and brings membership inference into the deep learning era: learning what matters rather than designing it, with generalization through training diversity and scal...

topics.title: Opal: Private Memory for Personal AI
topics.summary: Personal AI systems increasingly retain long-term memory of user activity, including documents, emails, messages, meetings, and ambient recordings. Trusted hardware can keep this data private, but struggles to scale with a growing datastore. This pushes the data to external storage, which exposes retrieval access patterns that leak private information to the application provider. Oblivious RAM (ORAM) is a cryptographic primitive that can hide these patterns, but it requires a fixed access budget, precluding the query-dependent traversals that agentic memory systems rely on for accuracy. We...

topics.title: Prism: Policy Reuse via Interpretable Strategy Mapping in Reinforcement Learning
topics.summary: We present PRISM (Policy Reuse via Interpretable Strategy Mapping), a framework that grounds reinforcement learning agents' decisions in discrete, causally validated concepts and uses those concepts as a zero-shot transfer interface between agents trained with different algorithms. PRISM clusters each agent's encoder features into $K$ concepts via K-means. Causal intervention establishes that these concepts directly drive - not merely correlate with - agent behavior: overriding concept assignments changes the selected action in 69.4% of interventions ($p = 8.6 \times 10^{-86}$, 2500 interve...

She quickly surmised that someone had pulled performances of the songs she posted to YouTube, created […]

paper_spotlight.label: New Frontier Papers
paper_spotlight.description: Papers that appear to open a genuinely new direction, paradigm, or field.

items.title: Gradient Boosting within a Single Attention Layer
items.summary: Reframing attention as intra-layer boosting is a distinctive architectural idea that could seed a broader family of iterative-correction attention mechanisms.
items.spotlight_comment: Reframing attention as intra-layer boosting is a distinctive architectural idea that could seed a broader family of iterative-correction attention mechanisms.

items.title: Robust Learning with Optimal Error
items.summary: The result appears to substantially revise optimal-error understanding in adversarial-noise learning by elevating randomized hypotheses from a technical detail to the key principle.
items.spotlight_comment: The result appears to substantially revise optimal-error understanding in adversarial-noise learning by elevating randomized hypotheses from a technical detail to the key principle.

paper_spotlight.label: Daily Hot Papers
paper_spotlight.description: Papers that feel broadly important to the day and belong in the hotspot paper feed.

items.title: VibeVoice Technical Report
items.summary: This report presents VibeVoice, a novel model designed to synthesize long-form speech with multiple speakers by employing next-token diffusion, which is a unified method for modeling continuous data by autoregressively generating latent vectors via diffusion. To enable this, we introduce a novel continuous speech tokenizer that, when compared to the popular Encodec model, improves data compression by 80 times while maintaining comparable performance. The tokenizer effectively preserves audio fidelity while significantly boosting computational efficiency for processing long sequences. Thus,...
items.spotlight_comment: This report presents VibeVoice, a novel model designed to synthesize long-form speech with multiple speakers by employing next-token diffusion, which is a unified method for modeling continuous data by autoregressively generating latent vectors via diffusion. To enable this, we introduce a novel...

items.title: PaddleOCR-VL: Boosting Multilingual Document Parsing via a 0.9B Ultra-Compact Vision-Language Model
items.summary: In this report, we propose PaddleOCR-VL, a SOTA and resource-efficient model tailored for document parsing. Its core component is PaddleOCR-VL-0.9B, a compact yet powerful vision-language model (VLM) that integrates a NaViT-style dynamic resolution visual encoder with the ERNIE-4.5-0.3B language model to enable accurate element recognition. This innovative model efficiently supports 109 languages and excels in recognizing complex elements (e.g., text, tables, formulas, and charts), while maintaining minimal resource consumption. Through comprehensive evaluations on widely used public benchm...
items.spotlight_comment: In this report, we propose PaddleOCR-VL, a SOTA and resource-efficient model tailored for document parsing. Its core component is PaddleOCR-VL-0.9B, a compact yet powerful vision-language model (VLM) that integrates a NaViT-style dynamic resolution visual encoder with the ERNIE-4.5-0.3B language...

items.title: Generative World Renderer
items.summary: Scaling generative inverse and forward rendering to real-world scenarios is bottlenecked by the limited realism and temporal coherence of existing synthetic datasets. To bridge this persistent domain gap, we introduce a large-scale, dynamic dataset curated from visually complex AAA games. Using a novel dual-screen stitched capture method, we extracted 4M continuous frames (720p/30 FPS) of synchronized RGB and five G-buffer channels across diverse scenes, visual effects, and environments, including adverse weather and motion-blur variants. This dataset uniquely advances bidirectional renderi...

items.title: AgentScope 1.0: A Developer-Centric Framework for Building Agentic Applications
items.summary: Driven by rapid advancements of Large Language Models (LLMs), agents are empowered to combine intrinsic knowledge with dynamic tool use, greatly enhancing their capacity to address real-world tasks. In line with such an evolution, AgentScope introduces major improvements in a new version (1.0), towards comprehensively supporting flexible and efficient tool-based agent-environment interactions for building agentic applications. Specifically, we abstract foundational components essential for agentic applications and provide unified interfaces and extensible modules, enabling developers to eas...
items.spotlight_comment: Driven by rapid advancements of Large Language Models (LLMs), agents are empowered to combine intrinsic knowledge with dynamic tool use, greatly enhancing their capacity to address real-world tasks. In line with such an evolution, AgentScope introduces major improvements in a new version (1.0), t...

items.title: Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with PagedAttention
items.summary: High throughput serving of large language models (LLMs) requires batching sufficiently many requests at a time. However, existing systems struggle because the key-value cache (KV cache) memory for each request is huge and grows and shrinks dynamically. When managed inefficiently, this memory can be significantly wasted by fragmentation and redundant duplication, limiting the batch size. To address this problem, we propose PagedAttention, an attention algorithm inspired by the classical virtual memory and paging techniques in operating systems. On top of it, we build vLLM, an LLM serving sys...
items.spotlight_comment: High throughput serving of large language models (LLMs) requires batching sufficiently many requests at a time. However, existing systems struggle because the key-value cache (KV cache) memory for each request is huge and grows and shrinks dynamically. When managed inefficiently, this memory can...

items.title: Very Large-Scale Multi-Agent Simulation in AgentScope
items.summary: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have opened new avenues for applying multi-agent systems in very large-scale simulations. However, there remain several challenges when conducting multi-agent simulations with existing platforms, such as limited scalability and low efficiency, unsatisfied agent diversity, and effort-intensive management processes. To address these challenges, we develop several new features and components for AgentScope, a user-friendly multi-agent platform, enhancing its convenience and flexibility for supporting very large-scale multi-agent simulations. Spec...

However, there remain several challenges when conducting multi-agent simulations with existing platforms, such as limited scalability and low efficiency, unsat...

x_buzz.title: Google AI Edge Gallery
x_buzz.summary:
Google AI Edge Gallery

Terrible name, really great app: this is Google's official app for running their Gemma 4 models (the E2B and E4B sizes, plus some members of the Gemma 3 family) directly on your iPhone.

It works really well. The E2B model is a 2.54GB download and is both fast and genuinely useful.

The app also provides "ask questions about images" and audio transcription (up to 30s) with the two small Gemma 4 models, and has an interesting "skills" demo which demonstrates tool calling against eight different interactive widgets, each implemented as an HTML page (though sadly the source code is not visible): interactive-map, kitchen-adventure, calculate-has...
